title: Ubuntu Linux
description: Complete command reference from beginner to advanced

commands.name: ls - List Directory
rows:
john@nc:~$ ls
Desktop  Documents  Downloads  photos  script.sh

john@nc:~$ ls -la
total 32
drwxr-xr-x 5 john john 4096 May 14 10:00 .
drwxr-xr-x 3 root root 4096 May 14 09:00 ..
-rw-r--r-- 1 john john  220 May 14 10:00 .bashrc
Sizes as K/M/G instead of bytes

john@nc:~$ ls -lh
total 1.2M
-rw-r--r-- 1 john john 1.2M May 14 video.mp4

john@nc:~$ ls -lS
-rw-r--r-- 1 john john 5.0M May 14 big.zip
-rw-r--r-- 1 john john 2.0K May 14 small.txt

john@nc:~$ ls -ltr
-rw-r--r-- 1 john john 0 Jan 1  2024 old.txt
-rw-r--r-- 1 john john 0 May 14 12:00 new.txt

commands.name: mkdir - Make Directory
rows:
john@nc:~$ ls
notes.txt  photo.jpg

john@nc:~$ mkdir projects
john@nc:~$ ls
notes.txt  photo.jpg  projects

john@nc:~$ mkdir -p a/b/c
john@nc:~$ tree a
a
  b
      c

2 directories, 0 files
Creates nested tree in one command

john@nc:~$ mkdir -p company/{engineering/{backend,frontend},hr} && touch company/README.md
john@nc:~$ tree company
company
  engineering
      backend
      frontend
  hr
  README.md

commands.name: rm - Remove Files/Dirs
rows:
john@nc:~$ ls
file.txt  notes.txt

john@nc:~$ rm file.txt
john@nc:~$ ls
notes.txt

john@nc:~$ ls
index.js  node_modules  package.json

john@nc:~$ rm -r node_modules
john@nc:~$ ls
index.js  package.json

john@nc:~$ rm -ri ./folder
rm - descend into directory './folder'? y
rm - remove regular empty file './folder/file.txt'? y
rm - remove directory './folder'? y

john@nc:~$ rm -rf .git
john@nc:~$ ls -la | grep .git
(no output, .git is gone)

commands.name: cp - Copy
rows:
john@nc:~$ ls
file.txt  notes.txt

john@nc:~$ cp file.txt backup.txt
john@nc:~$ ls
backup.txt  file.txt  notes.txt

john@nc:~$ ls
file.txt  project

john@nc:~$ cp -r project/ /backup/
john@nc:~$ ls /backup/
project

john@nc:~$ ls
file.txt  notes.txt

john@nc:~$ cp -u *.txt ~/backup/
john@nc:~$ ls ~/backup/
file.txt  notes.txt

commands.name: mv - Move / Rename
rows:
john@nc:~$ ls
file.txt  notes.txt

john@nc:~$ mv file.txt old.txt
john@nc:~$ ls
notes.txt  old.txt

john@nc:~$ mv .env.example .env
john@nc:~$ ls -la .env
-rw-r--r-- 1 john john 120 May 14 10:00 .env

commands.name: uname / echo / clear / history
rows:
john@nc:~$ uname -a
Linux john-pc 6.8.0-45-generic #46-Ubuntu SMP x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux

john@nc:~$ history
  1  ls
  2  cd projects
  3  git init
  4  npm install

commands.name: touch - Create/Update File
rows:
john@nc:~$ touch newfile.txt
john@nc:~$ ls -l newfile.txt
-rw-r--r-- 1 john john 0 May 14 10:00 newfile.txt

john@nc:~$ touch existing.txt
john@nc:~$ ls -l existing.txt
-rw-r--r-- 1 john john 0 May 14 10:01 existing.txt

john@nc:~$ touch -t 202501011200 f.txt
john@nc:~$ ls -l f.txt
-rw-r--r-- 1 john john 0 Jan  1  2025 f.txt

commands.name: cat - View File
rows:
john@nc:~$ cat file.txt
Hello World
This is a text file.
Line 3 here.

john@nc:~$ cat f1.txt f2.txt > combined.txt
john@nc:~$ cat combined.txt
(contents of both files combined)

commands.name: head / tail - View File Parts
rows:
john@nc:~$ head -n 3 file.txt
line 1
line 2
line 3

commands.name: find - Search Files
rows:
john@nc:~$ find . -name "*.tmp" -delete
john@nc:~$ find . -name "*.tmp"
(no output, no .tmp files found)

commands.name: chmod - Change Permissions
rows:
john@nc:~$ ls -l script.sh
-rw-r--r-- 1 john john 250 May 14 09:00 script.sh

john@nc:~$ chmod 755 script.sh
john@nc:~$ ls -l script.sh
-rwxr-xr-x 1 john john 250 May 14 09:00 script.sh

john@nc:~$ ls -l file.txt
-rwxr-xr-x 1 john john 100 May 14 09:00 file.txt

john@nc:~$ chmod 644 file.txt
john@nc:~$ ls -l file.txt
-rw-r--r-- 1 john john 100 May 14 09:00 file.txt

john@nc:~$ chmod 600 ~/.ssh/id_rsa
john@nc:~$ ls -l ~/.ssh/id_rsa
-rw------- 1 john john 2602 May 14 10:00 /home/john/.ssh/id_rsa

john@nc:~$ chmod u+x script.sh
john@nc:~$ ls -l script.sh
-rwxr--r-- 1 john john 0 May 14 10:00 script.sh

john@nc:~$ chmod -R 755 web/
john@nc:~$ ls -ld web/
drwxr-xr-x 2 john john 4096 May 14 10:00 web/

commands.name: chown - Change Owner
rows:
john@nc:~$ ls -l file.txt
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 100 May 14 09:00 file.txt

john@nc:~$ sudo chown john file.txt
john@nc:~$ ls -l file.txt
-rw-r--r-- 1 john root 100 May 14 09:00 file.txt

john@nc:~$ ls -l file.txt
-rw-r--r-- 1 john root 100 May 14 09:00 file.txt

john@nc:~$ sudo chown john:dev file.txt
john@nc:~$ ls -l file.txt
-rw-r--r-- 1 john dev 100 May 14 09:00 file.txt

john@nc:~$ sudo chown -R john:john /home/john
john@nc:~$ ls -ld /home/john
drwxr-xr-x 5 john john 4096 May 14 10:00 /home/john

commands.name: id / whoami / groups / passwd
rows: All groups a user belongs to

john@nc:~$ groups john
john  - john sudo docker

john@nc:~$ sudo passwd dev1
New password - 
Retype new password - 
passwd - password updated successfully

john@nc:~$ sudo passwd -l dev1
passwd - password expiry information changed.

john@nc:~$ sudo useradd -m -s /bin/bash dev1
john@nc:~$ getent passwd dev1
dev1:x:1001:1001::/home/dev1:/bin/bash

john@nc:~$ sudo usermod -aG docker john
john@nc:~$ groups john
john  - john sudo docker

john@nc:~$ sudo userdel -r dev1
john@nc:~$ getent passwd dev1
(no output, user deleted)

commands.name: apt - Update & Upgrade
rows: Fetch latest package info from repos

john@nc:~$ sudo apt update
Hit:1 http://archive.ubuntu.com jammy InRelease
Hit:2 http://archive.ubuntu.com jammy-updates InRelease
Reading package lists... Done

john@nc:~$ sudo apt upgrade -y
Reading package lists... Done
Building dependency tree... Done
0 upgraded, 0 newly installed, 0 to remove

commands.name: apt - Install & Remove
rows:
john@nc:~$ sudo apt install nginx -y
Reading package lists... Done
Setting up nginx (1.24.0) ...
Created symlink ...
Starting nginx... done.

john@nc:~$ apt search "web server"
nginx/stable 1.24.0 amd64
  high-performance web server
apache2/stable 2.4.57 amd64
  Apache HTTP Server

john@nc:~$ apt show nginx
Package - nginx
Version - 1.24.0
Priority - optional
Section - web
Installed-Size - 1,024 kB

john@nc:~$ sudo apt purge nginx
Reading package lists... Done
Removing nginx (1.24.0) ...
Purging configuration files...

john@nc:~$ sudo apt autoremove
0 upgraded, 0 newly installed, 3 to remove
Freed disk space - 42.5 MB

commands.name: ps - Process Status
rows:
john@nc:~$ ps aux
USER       PID %CPU %MEM    VSZ   RSS TTY      STAT START   TIME COMMAND
root         1  0.0  0.5  50000 12000 ?        Ss   May14   0:02 /sbin/init
john      1234  0.3  1.2 500000 30000 ?        Ssl  May14   0:15 node server.js
john      5678  2.0  8.0 2.5G 250000 ?        Sl   10:00   0:45 chrome

john@nc:~$ ps aux | grep nginx
root      1000  0.0  0.5  30000 12000 ?        Ss   May14   0:00 nginx - master
www-data  1001  0.0  0.3  30000  8000 ?        S    May14   0:00 nginx - worker

john@nc:~$ ps aux --sort=-%mem | head -3
USER       PID %CPU %MEM    VSZ   RSS COMMAND
john      5678  2.0  8.0 2.5G 250000 chrome
john      1234  0.3  1.2 500K  30000 node

commands.name: kill - Terminate Processes
rows:
john@nc:~$ kill 1234
john@nc:~$ ps -p 1234
  PID TTY          TIME CMD
(no output - process terminated)

john@nc:~$ kill -9 1234
john@nc:~$ ps -p 1234
  PID TTY          TIME CMD
(no output - process killed)

john@nc:~$ killall node
john@nc:~$ pgrep node
(no output - no node processes)

john@nc:~$ pkill -f "python server.py"
[1]+  Terminated              python server.py

commands.name: systemctl - Service Manager
rows:
john@nc:~$ systemctl status ssh
.service - OpenSSH Daemon
     Loaded - loaded (/lib/systemd/system/ssh.service; enabled)
     Active - active (running) since 2026-05-14 09:00:00 UTC
   Main PID - 1000 (sshd)
     Memory - 5.2M

john@nc:~$ sudo systemctl restart nginx
john@nc:~$ systemctl is-active nginx
active

john@nc:~$ sudo systemctl enable --now docker
Synchronizing state of docker.service with SysV service script...

-- Logs begin at May 14 09:00:00. --
May 14 10:00:01 host nginx[1000] - starting nginx ...

-- Logs begin at May 14 09:00:00. --
May 14 09:05:12 kernel - error -110
May 14 09:05:15 network - Failed to start Network Manager

commands.name: Background Jobs
rows:
john@nc:~$ fg %1
sleep 100
(process comes to foreground)

john@nc:~$ nohup python server.py > server.log 2>&1 &
[2] 12346
nohup - ignoring input and appending output to 'server.log'

commands.name: ip / ping
rows:
john@nc:~$ ip a
1 - lo - <LOOPBACK,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65536
    inet 127.0.0.1/8 scope host lo
2 - eth0 - <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 1500
    inet 192.168.1.100/24 scope global eth0

john@nc:~$ ip route
default via 192.168.1.1 dev eth0
192.168.1.0/24 dev eth0  proto kernel  scope link  src 192.168.1.100

john@nc:~$ ping -c 4 google.com
PING google.com (142.250.80.46) 56(84) bytes of data.
64 bytes from 142.250.80.46 - icmp_seq=1 ttl=116 time=12.3 ms
64 bytes from 142.250.80.46 - icmp_seq=2 ttl=116 time=11.8 ms

--- ping statistics ---
4 packets transmitted, 4 received, 0% packet loss

commands.name: curl - HTTP Client
rows:
john@nc:~$ curl -I https://example.com
HTTP/2 200
content-type - text/html; charset=UTF-8
server - nginx
content-length - 1256

john@nc:~$ curl -O https://example.com/file.zip
  % Total    % Received % Xferd  Average Speed
100 1024k  100 1024k    0     0   500k      0  0:00:02

commands.name: ss / ufw / dig
rows:
john@nc:~$ ss -tuln
Netid  State   Recv-Q  Send-Q  Local Address:Port
tcp    LISTEN  0       128     0.0.0.0:22
tcp    LISTEN  0       511     0.0.0.0:80

john@nc:~$ sudo ufw status
Status - active

To                         Action      From
--                         ------      ----
22/tcp                     ALLOW       Anywhere
80/tcp                     ALLOW       Anywhere

commands.name: grep - Search Text
rows:
john@nc:~$ grep "error" log.txt
[2026-05-14] ERROR - Connection refused
[2026-05-14] ERROR - Timeout exceeded

john@nc:~$ grep -r "TODO" --include="*.py" .
./src/main.py:5 - # TODO - add error handling

john@nc:~$ grep -A2 -B2 "Exception" server.log
2026-05-14 10:00:00 [INFO] processing request
2026-05-14 10:00:01 [ERROR] Exception - NullPointerException
2026-05-14 10:00:01 [WARN] retrying request

john@nc:~$ ps aux | grep nginx
root      1000  0.0  0.5  30000 12000 ?   Ss  May14  nginx - master

commands.name: awk / sed / sort / wc
rows:
john@nc:~$ sed -n '2,4p' file.txt
line 2
line 3
line 4

commands.name: tar - Archive Tool
rows:
john@nc:~$ tar -czvf archive.tar.gz myfolder/
myfolder/
myfolder/file1.txt
myfolder/file2.txt

john@nc:~$ tar -xzvf archive.tar.gz
myfolder/
myfolder/file1.txt
myfolder/file2.txt

john@nc:~$ tar -tzvf archive.tar.gz
drwxr-xr-x john/john       0 2026-05-14 myfolder/
-rw-r--r-- john/john    1024 2026-05-14 file1.txt

john@nc:~$ zip -r archive.zip myfolder/
  adding - myfolder/ (stored 0%)
  adding - myfolder/file1.txt (deflated 45%)

john@nc:~$ unzip -l archive.zip
Archive -  archive.zip
  Length      Date    Time    Name
     1024  2026-05-14 10:00   myfolder/file1.txt
     2048  2026-05-14 10:00   myfolder/file2.txt
     3072                     2 files

john@nc:~$ gzip -k file.txt
john@nc:~$ ls -l file.txt*
-rw-r--r-- 1 john john 1024 May 14 10:00 file.txt
-rw-r--r-- 1 john john  512 May 14 10:00 file.txt.gz

commands.name: Scripting Patterns
rows: for f in *.txt; do echo "$f"; done

john@nc:~$ for f in *.txt; do echo "$f"; done
file1.txt
file2.txt
notes.txt

john@nc:~$ output=$(ls -la); echo "$output"
total 12
drwxr-xr-x 2 john john 4096 May 14 10:00 .

commands.name: Pipe & Redirection
rows:
john@nc:~$ ls -la | grep ".txt"
-rw-r--r-- 1 john john    0 May 14 10:00 file.txt

john@nc:~$ ls > files.txt && cat files.txt
Desktop
Documents
Downloads
file.txt

john@nc:~$ echo "error" >&2
error
(printed to stderr, not stdout)

john@nc:~$ mkdir test && cd test
john@nc:~/test$

john@nc:~$ cd /nonexistent || echo "Not found"
bash - cd - /nonexistent - No such file or directory
Not found

commands.name: System Monitoring
rows:
john@nc:~$ df -h
Filesystem      Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on
/dev/sda1       100G   45G   55G  45% /
/dev/sdb1       500G  200G  300G  40% /data

john@nc:~$ free -h
               total        used        free      shared  buff/cache   available
Mem -            16G          8G         4G         1G         4G         10G
Swap -           2G          0G         2G

john@nc:~$ uptime
 10:00:00 up 5 days,  2:30,  2 users,  load average - 0.50, 0.30, 0.10

john@nc:~$ lsof -i :3000
COMMAND   PID  USER   FD   TYPE  DEVICE SIZE/OFF NODE NAME
node     12345 john   12u  IPv4  54321      0t0  TCP *:3000 (LISTEN)

john@nc:~$ last -5
john     pts/0        May 14 09:00   still logged in
john     pts/1        May 13 18:00 - 20:00  (02:00)
reboot   system boot  May 13 07:55   still running
